steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that Winnemucca addresses readers to establish her authority and credibility. The correct quotation must actively assert her knowledge or trustworthiness — not merely greet readers, express uncertainty, or describe events.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "My dear reader, I have not lived in this world for over thirty or forty years for nothing, and I know what I am talking about.": directly asserts her experience-based knowledge and credibility. ✓
  • Choice B — "I was born somewhere near 1844, but am not sure of the precise time.": expresses uncertainty about her own birth — the opposite of establishing authority. ✗
  • Choice C — "Oh, dear readers, these soldiers had gone only sixty miles away to Muddy Lake, where my people were then living and fishing.": narrates an event; addresses readers but does not assert authority or credibility. ✗
  • Choice D — "But how can I describe the scene that followed? Some of you, dear readers, can imagine.": acknowledges an inability to describe — undermines rather than establishes authority. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
